title: approvals — HTTP routes
description: Two route groups cover the full CRUD + decision + preflight + audit surface. Tenant-plane routes serve four sub-audiences: (1) any tenant user (viewing approvable-actions), (2) tenant admins (template CRUD + preflight), (3) requesters (instance list + view + withdraw), (4) approvers (pending-my-review + approve + reject). Platform-plane routes are read-only cross-tenant compliance + registry-management surfaces — decisions stay on the tenant plane.

groups.description: Tenant-plane approval-engine surface. `X-Application-Id` header required on every route; middleware refuses 400/404/403 on missing/unknown/disabled application.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.actions.index
routes.description: Lists approvable_actions the caller's tenant has active templates on. Used by (a) the requester UI to explain 'why did this need approval?' and (b) the template-authoring UI to pick which action a new template gates.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.store
routes.description: Author a new approval template. Body includes the template's basic fields + a groups[] array of CreateApprovalTemplateApproverInput (each with selector_expression + quorum). Body validated via valid_expression + valid_selector_expression + template_active_uniqueness. Consumes 1 unit of approval_templates_max_active entitlement.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.update
routes.description: Update a template. Semantic-behaviour field changes (when_expression, on_timeout, allows_recall, expires_after_hours) trigger a version bump — the returned row is the NEW row (version+1); the caller's original row is now is_active=false. Non-semantic field changes update in place.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.activate
routes.description: Flip is_active=true. The observer deactivates any peer with same (tenant, application, action_key, name).

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.destroy
routes.description: Soft-delete. Refused when approval_instances references this template with status='pending'.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.groups.index
routes.description: List approver groups on the template ordered by sort_order ASC.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.groups.store
routes.description: Add a group. Triggers ApprovalTemplateGroupsUpdated + ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped in the parent template's transaction.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.templates.preflight
routes.description: Dry-run a template's expression + selector groups against a synthetic context. NEVER persists — this is a compile + evaluate pass only. Powers the template-authoring UI's 'preview approvers' + 'test rule' features.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.instances.index
routes.description: List the caller's own submitted requests. Default filter: requester_id=caller.id (admin can drop with filter[requester_id]=*).

routes.name: tenant.approvals.instances.withdraw
routes.description: Requester withdraws their own pending instance. Refused on terminal states + when template.allows_recall=false.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.instances.pending-my-review
routes.description: Approvers' inbox — instances where the caller is in resolved_approvers on at least one PENDING requirement. Backed by a JSONB containment query (`resolved_approvers @> to_jsonb(auth_user.id)`) using the GIN index on approval_requirements.resolved_approvers.

queryParams.name: filter[urgency]
queryParams.description: 'expiring_soon' filters to instances with expires_at within 24h.

routes.name: tenant.approvals.instances.requirements.approve
routes.description: Approver's approve decision. Body: comment (optional, max 1000). Fires ApprovalDecisionRecorded → QuorumEvaluator → possibly ApprovalRequirementSatisfied → possibly ApprovalGranted (which SYNC-dispatches ApprovalInstanceExecutor).
errors: SELF_APPROVAL_REFUSED (403 — when requester === approver AND template group.allows_self_approve=false)

routes.name: tenant.approvals.instances.requirements.reject
routes.description: Approver's reject decision. Body: comment (required, min 10 chars — SOC 2 evidence).

groups.description: Platform-plane read + registry-management surface. No decision endpoints — approve/reject stay on the tenant plane where the caller's tenant + guard are unambiguous.

routes.name: platform.approvals.instances.index
routes.description: Cross-tenant instance list. Compliance auditor + support tooling. Never mutates.

routes.name: platform.approvals.audit-report
routes.description: Async — dispatches AuditReportGenerationJob. SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 A.9.2 evidence. Consumer polls the job or waits for the completion notification with the signed URL.

routes.name: platform.approvals.actions.index
routes.description: Full registry read — every approvable_action row across all namespaces.

routes.name: platform.approvals.actions.store
routes.description: Manual registry-entry creation. Break-glass for actions NOT discovered at boot (e.g. a hot-loaded module). Normally the seeder + discovery pass covers this.

routes.name: platform.approvals.actions.update
routes.description: Toggle is_active + edit label/description. action_key is immutable (natural key).

notes: Every tenant-plane route requires the `X-Application-Id` header (missing → 400 missing_application, unknown → 404 unknown_application, disabled → 403 application_disabled). Enforced by resolve.application middleware.

The `approve` middleware is NOT applied at this route-group level — it's applied to routes ON OTHER MODULES that carry #[AsApprovableAction] (billing.refunds.issue, access.grants.create, etc). This module's routes work directly on already-existing approval_instances.
No public host — approval-engine surfaces are strictly authenticated. The webhook subscription mechanism (for tenant SIEM feeds) publishes at broadcast + webhook layers, not HTTP-poll.
Rate limits: tenant plane `approvals` limiter = 60 requests/minute per user; platform plane `approvals-platform` = 300/minute per platform admin. Both configured in foundation::rate-limits.php.
The pending-my-review query hits a GIN index on approval_requirements.resolved_approvers — the query plan uses `resolved_approvers @> ?::jsonb` containment which the GIN supports natively.

Wire response envelope: every non-204 response is wrapped `{ data: ..., meta?: paginated_meta }`. The 202 responses for withdraw / async job dispatch return `{ data: { instance_id, status } }` / `{ data: { report_generation_job_id, ... } }`.
